Website Redesign // 2026.06
New Tech Machinery · Website Rebuild · June 2026

Built to guide buyers from first look to confident purchase.

A site organized around the buyer's journey: the strategy, the features, and what's next.

The problem

The old site got in the buyer's way.

01Navigation
  • Organized like our org chart: Machines, Resources, Learning Center
  • Not the questions a buyer actually asks
02Finding content
  • No fast way to land on a manual, spec, or video
  • Search was weak, so people gave up or called
03Look & feel
  • Crowded and dense, too many competing colors
  • An older feel that didn't look like the industry leaders we are
04Mobile
  • Owners hunt for manuals and support on a phone; buyers and learners are on desktop
  • The old site wasn't built for either job well
The big idea

Four labels. Four moments in the buyer's journey.

01 Aware New to Roll Forming? Never owned a machine. Make the case.
→
02 Consider Choose Your Machine Compare models, profiles, and tooling.
→
03 Decide How to Buy Quote, pricing, financing. Close the deal.
→
04 Own Get Owner Support Manuals, firmware, service. Backed after.
The framework · StoryBrand / Endless Customers

The contractor is the hero. We're the guide.

Every section casts the buyer as the one taking action and growing their business. NTM is the expert who hands them a clear plan, not a sales pitch. This isn't a layer we added. It's how the copy is written.

  • →Hero: the contractor, keeping their own margin
  • →Guide: NTM, with a plan and the proof
  • →Plan: Explore · Build · Finance
  • →Stakes: profit you give away vs. profit you keep

“The machine you pick is the one we build.” — live homepage copy, the “how you buy” section

Feature · Finance Center

Financing was a black hole. Now it's a guided router.

Every way to pay, in one place. We even tell them they can use their own bank.

01 · FastestApply with CorbelSoft pull, decision in 4–8 hrs.
02 · TaxSection 179Deduct up to $1.22M.
03 · LenderFinance through a bankFNB preferred + a real directory.
04 · YoursBring your own lenderUse any bank you trust.
Feature · The buying funnel

Build · Quote · Finance. One flow, no gatekeeping.

The configurator · build, price, and apply in the browser

  • 01Build: spec the machine in a guided picker.
  • 02Quote: a real, itemized price. No “contact us for pricing.”
  • 03Finance: apply in the same flow, all in the browser.

We put this on the machine page, not the homepage — on purpose. — fewer quotes, but far better-qualified ones

Feature · Navigation & mobile

Mobile-first. The same map, whatever the screen.

Desktop · machine cards live in the menu

  • →Same four labels as desktop: one mental model
  • →Big touch targets, built for a thumb in the field
  • →Deliberate motion: one panel closes before the next opens
  • →Accessible: screen-reader announced, keyboard-driven
The look & feel

Industrial. Engineered. Built like the machines.

Machine page · real Q3 photography, engineered chrome

  • —Sharp corners, built like the machines, not a soft consumer app
  • —One disciplined palette: restraint reads as engineered
  • —A mono typeface that tags the spec data: the B2B signal
  • —Real photos of real machines on real jobs, not AI

Every choice maps to the brand — it's a system, not taste. — answer "why," not "do you like it"

Also shipped

More that makes the site work harder.

Service Hub · content-first, per-machine support

Learning Center · the educational engine

DiscoveryLive header searchInstant results, top-5, filtered by type. Find a manual in two seconds.
OwnershipService HubPick a machine → its manuals, firmware, troubleshooting, training.
FoundationMachine data layerOne source of truth drives specs, pricing, and finance everywhere.
The ask

Two questions, and what comes next.

01
Is the direction right?
02
Will you give me the content & people to finish it?
  • Road to go-live · first week of July
  • → Pillar-page content review: Rick
  • → Train sales & service: live + video (Rick schedules)
  • → Finance accuracy review: Kathy + Terry
  • → Short content-freeze for migration + redirects
  • → Direct service requests (NetSuite): phase 2
What's next · Phase 2 & beyond

The launch is the foundation. Here's where it goes.

01Sharpen buying
Compare toolApple-style side-by-side machine specs, so buyers decide with confidence.
Offer bannersPromote “0% financing” or a featured machine right in the nav and on machine pages.
02Deepen ownership
Parts e-commerceOwners buy small replacement parts directly. No service ticket, less backlog.
Direct service requestsCreate a NetSuite case from the site, skipping the HubSpot middle-step. Gated on source-tracking.
03Future platform
“Sally” support botA guided assistant + smart article CTAs for buyers who want to move faster.
Mobile appThe longer arc: owners manage service and parts from their phone in the field.
